title: Framework Integration with Build Tools Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the critical role of build tools in modern frontend development.

questions.question: What is the primary function of a build tool in frontend development?
options: To host the website on a public server.
To transform source code into browser-ready, optimized assets.
To generate graphics for the UI.
To write the HTML and CSS for you.

questions.question: Which tool has become a popular modern alternative to Webpack due to its speed?
options: Babel
Vite
Gulp
Grunt

questions.question: What is the purpose of Hot Module Replacement (HMR)?
options: To replace your entire application with a new version upon build.
To update only changed modules in the browser without a full reload during development.
To automatically minify all CSS files.
To handle database migrations.

questions.question: How do you typically integrate Tailwind CSS into a build tool like Vite or Webpack?
options: By adding it as a script tag in your HTML.
Using PostCSS and a postcss.config.js file.
By writing all your CSS as inline styles.
It's built into all browsers by default.

questions.question: What does 'Tree Shaking' refer to in the context of build tools?
options: Automatically adding new features to your project.
Removing unused code from your final bundles.
Optimizing the file structure of your project.
Compressing images for faster load.
